name: hero-video-section
role: Background-video marketing hero. Hero category 5 of 5 — the highest-bandwidth, highest-attention-cost variant. Auto-playing muted looping video behind the canonical hero anatomy. WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) requires user controls for any motion over 5 seconds; the hero-video-controls primitive provides the required pause/play affordance. Reduced-motion: video pauses on initial render and shows poster image instead (host JS handles the toggle).

registers.background-loop: Video fills the hero corner-to-corner. Muted, autoplay, loop, playsinline. The 80% choice for marketing flagship video heroes.
registers.embed-with-controls: Video rendered inline with native browser controls. Used when the video is the content (e.g., product demo embedded in hero). Controls are always visible; user starts playback explicitly.

wcag222.rule: WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) — any moving content that starts automatically AND lasts more than 5 seconds AND is presented in parallel with other content MUST have a user-controllable pause mechanism.
wcag222.implementation: hero-video-controls primitive renders a visible pause button. Default state shows pause icon (video is playing); after click, switches to play icon. The button has explicit aria-label naming the action ('Pause hero video' / 'Play hero video'). Focus-visible outline guaranteed.
wcag222.nonNegotiable: This is NOT optional. Auto-playing background video without a pause control is a WCAG 2.2.2 violation. The hero-video-controls primitive is part of the pattern's hard contract — host cannot omit it.

reducedMotionPolicy.rule: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, the video should NOT auto-play. Initial render shows the poster image; user must explicitly press play to start the video.
reducedMotionPolicy.implementation: Host JS detects window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches on mount and sets data-state='reduced-motion' on the section. CSS hides the playing-state animations; the video element gets paused=true. Poster image fills the video bounds.
reducedMotionPolicy.noOpacityHacks: Don't 'reduce' the video to 50% opacity or similar — pause it. Reduced motion means stop the motion entirely, not soften it.

meta.audioPolicy: Background-loop register MUST be muted (browser autoplay requires it). If audio is meaningful to the content, use the embed-with-controls register instead, with native browser audio controls.
meta.licenseNote: Video files are host-provided. The specimen uses CSS-animated placeholder fixtures to avoid licensing entanglement. Production hosts supply their own licensed video via <source src='...'>.

ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: <section aria-labelledby='...'> points to the h1's id.
ariaPatterns.videoAriaLabel: <video aria-label='...'> describes what the video shows (e.g., 'Time-lapse of designers using Galley Easel'). Used by AT to announce the video's content.

name: hero-video-media
role: Wrapper for the <video> element + its <source>s + poster fallback. Manages object-fit, object-position, and the loading lifecycle. Renders as <video> with autoplay+muted+loop+playsinline attributes for background-loop register; with native controls for embed-with-controls register.

semanticStructure: aria-label='Time-lapse of designers using Galley Easel'>
<!-- Fallback for browsers with no <video> support — poster image still renders -->
meta.muteIsRequired: muted attribute is REQUIRED for autoplay. Browsers block auto-playing video with audio unless the user explicitly grants permission. This pattern's contract assumes muted background loops.
meta.playsinlineIsRequired: playsinline attribute is REQUIRED on iOS to prevent the video from going fullscreen on play. Without it, mobile Safari shows the video in fullscreen mode.
meta.preloadMetadataNotAuto: preload='metadata' (not 'auto') — fetches dimensions + duration but not the full video data on initial load. The autoplay trigger then fetches the rest. Avoids LCP-blocking on slow connections.
meta.posterImage: Always provide a poster. The poster is what users see during video load AND when reduced-motion pauses playback AND when the video errors out. The poster IS the LCP candidate for this hero variant — apply <picture>+fetchpriority='high' logic to it (load the poster eagerly).
meta.videoFormats: WebM (vp9 codec) as primary; MP4 (h.264) as fallback. WebM is significantly smaller for the same quality. Most modern browsers support WebM; MP4 is universal.

name: hero-video-overlay
role: Optional gradient/tint overlay layered over the video for text-on-video contrast. Same 4 registers × 3 tones × 3 strengths as hero-brand-photo's hero-photo-overlay. Always aria-hidden — purely decorative contrast aid.

meta.sameAsBrandPhotoOverlay: Identical contract to hero-brand-photo's hero-photo-overlay. Implemented independently to keep hero packs self-contained (no cross-hero composition).

name: hero-video-controls
role: REQUIRED WCAG 2.2.2 pause control for auto-playing background-loop video. Renders as a small button positioned in a corner of the hero (default: bottom-right). Shows pause icon when video is playing, play icon when paused. Optional secondary mute toggle (for when audio is also enabled in the embed-with-controls register, though background-loop is always muted).

meta.ariaLabelStateAware: aria-label switches based on video state: 'Pause hero video' when playing, 'Play hero video' when paused. Update on toggle.
meta.iconStateAware: The icon inside also switches — pause glyph when playing, play glyph when paused. Both icons are aria-hidden (label carries meaning).
meta.alwaysVisible: The control is ALWAYS visible (not on-hover-only). WCAG 2.2.2 requires the control be discoverable without hover; non-discoverable controls fail the criterion.
meta.positioning: Fixed within the hero-section's coordinate space, not the viewport. Default bottom-right at 24px inset.
meta.keyboardActivation: Enter and Space activate. focus-visible outline visible.

ariaPatterns.wcag222Compliance: This primitive is the WCAG 2.2.2 pause/stop control. Pattern-level non-negotiable.
ariaPatterns.iconAriaHidden: Icon SVGs are aria-hidden; aria-label on the button carries meaning.

name: hero-video-eyebrow
role: Pre-heading above the headline. Same anatomy as hero-type-only's hero-eyebrow. Color shifts to --text-on-accent on video backgrounds.

meta.defaultOnVideo: Default tone for hero-video is 'on-video' (--text-on-accent) since the video is the background. Switch to 'neutral' only if the overlay is light-toned.

name: hero-video-headline
role: Display headline. h1 by default. Color defaults to --text-on-accent (white) for visibility against video backgrounds. Optional text-shadow for guaranteed contrast on busy video frames.

meta.textShadowPolicy: Default shadow=false relies on the overlay primitive for contrast. shadow=true adds a subtle text-shadow for guaranteed contrast on overlay='none' video heroes. Use sparingly — prefer overlay.

name: hero-video-subhead
role: Supporting subtitle. Color defaults to a softened --text-on-accent (90% opacity). Same contract as hero-photo-subhead.

name: hero-video-actions
role: CTA cluster composing button-system. Ghost-intent gets backdrop-blur + semi-transparent surface against video. Same technique as hero-gradient-mesh and hero-brand-photo.

meta.ghostOnVideo: Ghost CTA gets backdrop-filter: blur(8px) + color-mix(in oklch, --surface-elevated 30%, transparent) for legibility against motion.

name: hero-video-meta
role: Optional small-print row. Same 3 registers as hero-type-only's hero-meta. Color softened against video background.

meta.trustBadgeAltText: Trust-badge images carry alt-text naming the certification (per hero-type-only contract).
